How can I get admitted to ENSA Fès? Admission to ENSA Fès is done through the National Common Entrance Exam (CNC) for scientific baccalaureate holders or students who have completed preparatory classes (CPGE). Access to the Integrated Preparatory Cycle (CPI) is open to baccalaureate graduates with a 'Good' or 'Very Good' mention in Mathematics or Physics.

What baccalaureate is required to enter ENSA Fès? Applicants must hold a Moroccan scientific baccalaureate, preferably in Mathematics (SM-A or SM-B) or Physics (SP), with a 'Good' or 'Very Good' mention. Candidates from other scientific streams may also apply depending on conditions set annually by the Ministry of Higher Education.

What is the CNC and how does it relate to ENSA Fès admissions? The CNC (Concours National Commun) is the national competitive exam used by all ENSA schools in Morocco to select engineering students. Candidates who pass the written tests are invited to oral exams, after which they are ranked and assigned to their chosen school and program based on their score.

Can I join ENSA Fès through a parallel track without the CNC? Yes. ENSA Fès accepts students through a parallel admission track (admission sur titre) for holders of a DUT, BTS, or a university licence in a relevant scientific field. This track is subject to an available quota and a selection process based on academic records.

What is the Integrated Preparatory Cycle (CPI) at ENSA Fès? The Integrated Preparatory Cycle (CPI) is a two-year foundation program at ENSA Fès covering mathematics, physics, computer science, and languages. It is integrated within the school, unlike external CPGE classes, and prepares students for the internal entrance exam to the engineering cycle.

What documents are required for ENSA Fès admission? Required documents typically include: a copy of the national baccalaureate diploma, official transcripts, a copy of the national ID, passport-size photos, and proof of the CNC ranking or parallel track eligibility. Specific requirements are published annually on the official ENSA Fès website (ensaf.ac.ma).

When does the ENSA Fès application period open? The application period for ENSA Fès generally opens in the spring (April–June) following the national baccalaureate exam results. Exact dates are announced on the official ENSA Fès website and through the CNC platform. Prospective students should monitor the school's official channels closely.

Is there a minimum GPA to apply to ENSA Fès? There is no fixed minimum GPA, but in practice ENSA Fès is a selective school. Successful candidates for the CPI typically have a 'Good' or 'Very Good' baccalaureate mention (average above 14/20). For the engineering cycle via CNC, ranking among the top candidates is essential.

Does ENSA Fès accept international students? Yes, ENSA Fès can accept international students, particularly those from French-speaking African countries. International applicants must meet the academic requirements and may need to provide equivalency documents for their baccalaureate. They should contact the admissions office directly for guidance.

How many students are admitted to ENSA Fès each year? ENSA Fès admits approximately 300–400 new students each year across all programs and admission tracks. The precise quota for each specialization is announced at the start of the CNC cycle and varies according to capacity and program accreditation.

What scientific subjects are covered in the CNC written exam? The CNC written exam covers mathematics, physics, chemistry, and optionally computer science depending on the track. The exam is common to all ENSA schools in Morocco. Preparation materials and past papers are available on the Ministry of Higher Education website.

What is the ENSA Fès admissions website? The official ENSA Fès website is https://ensaf.ac.ma. Admission-related information, program descriptions, and application instructions are published there. Prospective students can also consult the student digital workspace at https://dws.ensaf.ac.ma for registered students.

Can students from CPGE (preparatory classes) apply directly to the ENSA Fès engineering cycle? Yes. Students who have completed two years of CPGE (Classes Préparatoires aux Grandes Écoles) can apply directly to the engineering cycle at ENSA Fès through the CNC. This bypasses the integrated preparatory cycle and allows them to enroll directly in the first year of the three-year engineering program.

Is ENSA Fès a public or private institution? ENSA Fès is a public engineering school. It is part of the Université Sidi Mohamed Ben Abdellah (USMBA) in Fès and operates under the supervision of the Moroccan Ministry of Higher Education, Scientific Research and Innovation. This makes it accessible at very low tuition costs.

What engineering specializations can I choose after the CPI at ENSA Fès? After completing the Integrated Preparatory Cycle, students choose from 11 engineering specializations: Computer Engineering, Networked Systems and Cybersecurity, Digital Development and Cybersecurity, Energy Engineering and Intelligent Systems, Industrial Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Mechatronics Engineering, Embedded Systems and AI, Data Science and AI Engineering, Software Engineering and AI, and IT Engineering with AI and Digital Trust.

What happens if I fail to obtain a place through the CNC? Students who do not secure a place through the CNC can consider the parallel admission track (for DUT, BTS, or licence holders), retaking the CNC the following year, or applying to other public universities or private institutions. The admissions office at ENSA Fès can provide guidance on available alternatives.

What is the role of the admissions office at ENSA Fès? The admissions office manages the entire enrollment process, including verifying application files, communicating with candidates, processing CNC results, and coordinating registration for admitted students. It also handles parallel track applications and international student inquiries. Contact can be made via the official email or phone number of ENSA Fès.

Is there an interview process for admission to ENSA Fès? The CNC process includes oral examinations for shortlisted candidates following the written tests. For parallel track admissions, a selection committee reviews academic records and may conduct interviews. There is no separate interview specific to ENSA Fès beyond the national CNC process.

Can I transfer from another Moroccan university to ENSA Fès? Transfers from other Moroccan universities to ENSA Fès are possible on a case-by-case basis, subject to available places and an evaluation of equivalences by the academic council. Candidates must typically hold a relevant licence or first-cycle diploma and apply through the parallel admission track.

What is the duration of the engineering program at ENSA Fès? The full engineering program at ENSA Fès lasts 5 years: 2 years in the Integrated Preparatory Cycle (CPI) covering fundamental sciences, followed by 3 years in the engineering cycle focused on the chosen specialization. Students entering directly through CNC after CPGE complete only the 3-year engineering cycle.

What is the minimum mention required on the baccalaureate to apply to ENSA Fès CPI? Applicants to the Integrated Preparatory Cycle must have obtained at least a 'Good' mention (mention Bien, average ≥ 14/20) or a 'Very Good' mention (mention Très Bien, average ≥ 16/20) in a scientific baccalaureate. This requirement may vary slightly from year to year based on the Ministry's directives.

Is the ENSA Fès engineering degree nationally recognized? Yes, the Engineer of State degree (Diplôme d'Ingénieur d'État) delivered by ENSA Fès is officially recognized by the Moroccan state and accredited by the National Coordination of Engineering Schools. It is equivalent to a Bac+5 level and opens doors to both public and private sector employment.

Can I apply to ENSA Fès after a DUT? Yes. Holders of a DUT (Diplôme Universitaire de Technologie) in a relevant technical field can apply to ENSA Fès through the parallel admission track. They enter directly into the first year of the engineering cycle (skipping the preparatory cycle) subject to the availability of places and the outcome of the selection committee.

Does ENSA Fès participate in the national orientation platform? Yes, ENSA Fès is integrated into the national higher education orientation platform managed by the Ministry. Newly admitted baccalaureate holders can find ENSA Fès listed among the available options and submit their preferences through the official orientation portal.

What is the academic level required to enter the engineering cycle at ENSA Fès directly? To enter the engineering cycle directly (without going through the CPI), candidates must have completed two years of CPGE or hold an equivalent Bac+2 qualification (DUT, BTS, Deug) in a relevant scientific or technical field. Entry is competitive and based on the CNC ranking or a selection committee for parallel tracks.

Is the number of places in each engineering specialization at ENSA Fès limited? Yes. Each specialization at ENSA Fès has a limited number of places (typically 30–40 students per cohort). Placement is competitive and determined by the student's ranking at the end of the CPI internal exam or by the CNC ranking for external CPGE candidates.

Does ENSA Fès offer preparatory cycle places to students from other cities? Yes, ENSA Fès is open to students from all regions of Morocco. For students from other cities, the school offers access to university housing (cité universitaire) and subsidized meals through the university restaurant. Applications for housing must be submitted at the beginning of the academic year.

What are the exam subjects tested in the ENSA Fès CPI internal entrance exam? At the end of the Integrated Preparatory Cycle, students sit an internal entrance exam to compete for places in the engineering specializations. The exam covers mathematics, physics, and often computer science, and is graded on both written exams and the overall GPA during the CPI years.

Is there a tuition fee for the ENSA Fès engineering program? As a public institution, ENSA Fès charges very low registration fees, typically a few hundred Moroccan Dirhams per year. This is in line with national policy for public higher education. Students may also be eligible for state scholarships based on merit or financial need.

What is USMBA and what is its relation to ENSA Fès? USMBA stands for Université Sidi Mohamed Ben Abdellah, one of Morocco's largest public universities, based in Fès. ENSA Fès is one of USMBA's constituent institutions. The university provides administrative and academic oversight, and ENSA Fès students hold a USMBA student card and benefit from all university services.

How can I prepare for the CNC exam to maximize my chances of entering ENSA Fès? The best preparation for the CNC includes mastering the baccalaureate-level mathematics and physics curricula, practicing past CNC exams available on the Ministry's website, and attending private tutoring sessions if needed. Strong performance in the baccalaureate, particularly in math and physics, is the most reliable predictor of success.

Does ENSA Fès offer any scholarships for incoming students? ENSA Fès does not directly award scholarships, but students may apply for state scholarships (bourses d'études) managed by the Ministry of Higher Education. These are awarded based on academic merit or financial need. Social support funds and housing assistance are also available through the university.

What is the language of instruction at ENSA Fès? The primary language of instruction at ENSA Fès is French, which is the standard for technical and scientific higher education in Morocco. English is also increasingly used, especially in computer science, AI, and data science programs. Arabic is used for certain non-technical modules.

What happens after I receive an admission offer from ENSA Fès? After receiving an admission offer, students must confirm their enrollment within the deadline provided, submit all required documents to the admissions office, pay the registration fee, and attend the orientation day at the start of the academic year. Late responses may result in losing the allocated place.

Is there a quota for SM-A and SM-B baccalaureate students at ENSA Fès? No fixed separate quota exists for SM-A vs SM-B students. Both qualify for the CPI admission process. In practice, SM-A students (Mathematics) tend to have a slight advantage due to the mathematical emphasis of the CNC. SP (Physics-Chemistry) students are also eligible and competitive.

Can I defer my admission to ENSA Fès for one year? Deferring admission at ENSA Fès is not standard practice. If a student cannot enroll in a given year, they would generally need to reapply the following year through the same CNC or parallel track process. Exceptional cases (serious illness, military service) may be considered on a case-by-case basis by the administration.

What is the selection rate for ENSA Fès admissions? ENSA Fès is a selective institution. The acceptance rate through the CNC is estimated at a few percent of total applicants nationally. The CPI internal exam is also competitive, with not all students automatically advancing to the engineering cycle. Exact selection rates are not published officially.

Does ENSA Fès accept BTS holders for parallel admission? Yes. Holders of a BTS (Brevet de Technicien Supérieur) in a relevant technical field can apply to ENSA Fès through the parallel admission track, subject to available places and a review of academic equivalences. They enter the first year of the engineering cycle directly.

What is the academic calendar for the first year of CPI at ENSA Fès? The first year of the Integrated Preparatory Cycle begins in September and consists of two semesters. Semester 1 runs from September to January (exams in January), and Semester 2 runs from February to June (exams in June). Catch-up (rattrapage) sessions follow each main exam period.

What is the official ENSA Fès website for admissions information? The official website is https://ensaf.ac.ma. It provides up-to-date information on admissions procedures, available specializations, required documents, and important dates. Prospective students are strongly advised to consult this site regularly during the application period.

What engineering programs does ENSA Fès offer? ENSA Fès offers 11 initial training engineering specializations: Computer Engineering (GI), Networked Systems and Cybersecurity (GSCSI), Digital Development and Cybersecurity (GDNC), Energy Engineering and Intelligent Systems (GESI), Industrial Engineering, Mechanical Engineering (GM), Mechatronics Engineering, Embedded Systems and AI (ISEAI), Data Science and AI Engineering (ISDAI), IT Engineering with AI and Digital Trust (IIIACN), and Software Engineering and AI (ILIA). All are preceded by a 2-year Integrated Preparatory Cycle.

How long is the engineering program at ENSA Fès? The full engineering program lasts 5 years: 2 years in the Integrated Preparatory Cycle (CPI) covering foundational sciences, followed by 3 years in the chosen engineering specialization. Students entering from CPGE go directly into the 3-year cycle.

What is the Computer Engineering (GI) program at ENSA Fès? The Computer Engineering program (Génie Informatique, GI) trains versatile engineers in software development, networks, databases, and artificial intelligence. The curriculum covers object-oriented programming, full-stack web development, advanced networking, cybersecurity, AI (machine learning and deep learning), big data, mobile development, embedded systems, and advanced software architectures. It is coordinated by Prof. BOULAALAM Abdelhak.

What modules are taught in the Computer Engineering program at ENSA Fès? The GI program spans 3 years. Year 1: OOP, networking fundamentals, databases, systems, mathematics for engineers, full-stack web development, Java modeling. Year 2: JEE, Python for AI, statistics, NoSQL, mobile development, IoT, business intelligence, machine learning and deep learning. Year 3: agile project management, software testing, big data, DevOps, software architectures (SOA, microservices).

What is the Networked Systems and Cybersecurity (GSCSI) program at ENSA Fès? The GSCSI program trains engineers specialized in networks, communication systems, and cybersecurity. It covers network fundamentals, Java and Python programming, web technologies, network and system security, intrusion detection, blockchain, mobile radio networks, antennas, microwaves, and satellite communications. It is coordinated by Prof. BALBOUL Younes.

What modules are covered in the GSCSI program at ENSA Fès? GSCSI Year 1: OS, network fundamentals, Java, databases, AI foundations. Year 2: network/system administration, web technologies, Java API, network services and cryptography, Python and AI, network security, enterprise networks, virtualization and cloud. Year 3: blockchain, microwaves, satellite communications, forensics and security auditing, optical communication systems, entrepreneurship.

What is the Digital Development and Cybersecurity (GDNC) program at ENSA Fès? The GDNC program trains expert engineers in secure software development and cybersecurity. It covers C++, Java, Python programming, web and mobile development, cybersecurity, cryptography and blockchain, ethical hacking, malware analysis, cloud computing, cloud and IoT security, containerization and DevSecOps, and security governance. Coordinated by Prof. JEGHAL Adil.

What is the Energy Engineering and Intelligent Systems (GESI) program at ENSA Fès? The GESI program trains engineers specializing in energy systems, renewable energy, and intelligent systems. The curriculum includes applied thermodynamics, heat transfer, electrical machines, power electronics, solar, wind, hydroelectric, nuclear, and green hydrogen energy, embedded systems, machine learning for energy, smart grids, intelligent vehicles, and energy auditing. Coordinated by Prof. HIHI Hicham.

What is the Industrial Engineering program at ENSA Fès? The Industrial Engineering program trains engineers to design, optimize, and manage industrial production systems. It covers robotics, lean manufacturing, supply chain management, production and inventory management, industrial processes and Industry 4.0, innovative materials, industrial regulation, reliability engineering, industrial maintenance, industrial ecology, and innovation. Coordinated by Prof. EL KHATTABI Souad.

What is the Mechanical Engineering (GM) program at ENSA Fès? The Mechanical Engineering program (Génie Mécanique) trains engineers in mechanical design, numerical simulation, and manufacturing. The curriculum covers strength of materials, fluid mechanics, heat transfer, structural analysis, mechatronics, innovative materials, AI in mechanics, finite element method, numerical aerodynamics, fracture mechanics, and automotive and aeronautical engineering. Coordinated by Prof. EL HAINI Jamila.

What is the Mechatronics Engineering program at ENSA Fès? The Mechatronics Engineering program combines mechanics, electronics, computer science, and control engineering to design complex systems. It covers digital electronics, microcontrollers, CAD, signal processing, robotics, embedded systems, computer vision, electrical machines, modern control, AI for mechatronics, and automotive and avionics embedded systems. Coordinated by Prof. SAYYOURI Mhamed.

What is the Embedded Systems and AI (ISEAI) program at ENSA Fès? The ISEAI program trains engineers specializing in designing intelligent embedded systems. It covers embedded processor architectures, electronic systems, power electronics, programmable circuits (FPGA, VHDL), signal processing, ARM processors, embedded Linux, trusted embedded AI, embedded vision, real-time systems, cybersecurity for embedded systems, and electric and autonomous vehicle technologies. Coordinated by Prof. ALAMI MARKTANI Malika.

What is the Data Science and AI Engineering (ISDAI) program at ENSA Fès? The ISDAI program trains data science and AI engineers. The curriculum covers Python for data science, machine learning, deep learning, big data, NoSQL, business intelligence, multi-agent systems, blockchain, cloud computing, computer vision, NLP, recommendation systems, reinforcement learning, and MLOps. Coordinated by Prof. EL AKKAD Nabil.

What is the IT Engineering with AI and Digital Trust (IIIACN) program at ENSA Fès? The IIIACN program trains engineers combining software development, AI, and digital security. It covers Java, Python, AI foundations, networking and system administration, cryptography, machine learning, deep learning, NLP, network security, cloud and DevOps, hacking techniques, blockchain, big data analytics, ERP, and web frameworks. Coordinated by Prof. BERRADA Mohammed.

What is the Software Engineering and AI (ILIA) program at ENSA Fès? The ILIA program trains expert engineers in software development and artificial intelligence. The curriculum covers Java OOP, frontend and backend web development, Python and AI frameworks, Oracle and NoSQL databases, machine learning and deep learning, reinforcement learning, computer vision, big data, NLP, generative AI, DevOps, business intelligence, and software testing. Coordinated by Prof. KHAMLICHI Youness.

What is the Integrated Preparatory Cycle (CPI) at ENSA Fès? The CPI lasts 2 years (4 semesters) and forms the common scientific foundation for all specializations. It covers mathematics (algebra, calculus, probability), physics (mechanics, thermodynamics, electromagnetism, optics), computer science (C, C++ programming, digital systems), languages (English and French), and transversal skills including entrepreneurship culture.

What subjects are taught in the first year of the ENSA Fès CPI? Year 1 of the CPI covers: Algebra 1 and 2, Calculus 1 and 2, Mechanics of a Point, Analog and Digital Electronics, Computer Science 1, English/French, Thermodynamics, Electrostatics and Magnetostatics, General Chemistry, Technical Drawing, C Programming, and Digital Skills.

What subjects are taught in the second year of the ENSA Fès CPI? Year 2 of the CPI covers: Calculus 3 and 4, Algebra 3, Numerical Analysis, Advanced C Programming, Solid Mechanics, Fluid Mechanics, Circuit Analysis (Electrocinetics), Statistics and Probability, Optics, Electromagnetism and Wave Physics, and Entrepreneurship Culture.

Does ENSA Fès offer continuing education programs? Yes. ENSA Fès offers continuing education through university licences and master's degrees. Available licences include: CRSI, FDWJEE, IAWM, IL, and M2I. Available masters include: 2ISD, CMPI, DLA, GLD, GLSI, IANT, IDIA, M2I, and SICN. These programs allow working professionals to specialize or upgrade their skills.

What is the GDNC curriculum year by year at ENSA Fès? GDNC Year 1: OOP C++, network fundamentals, OS, databases, mathematics for cybersecurity. Year 2: advanced system administration, cybersecurity, web and mobile development, cryptography and blockchain, embedded systems, virtual reality with AI. Year 3: ethical hacking, AI, malware attacks and analysis, cloud computing, advanced software architecture, cloud and IoT security, containerization and DevSecOps, security governance.

How many specializations are offered at ENSA Fès? ENSA Fès offers 11 engineering specializations in initial training: GI, GSCSI, GDNC, GESI, Génie Industriel, GM, Mécatronique, ISEAI, ISDAI, IIIACN, and ILIA. In addition, several continuing education licenses and master's programs are available. The total number of programs makes ENSA Fès one of the most diverse ENSA schools in Morocco.

What is the code of the ENSA Fès Computer Engineering program? The Computer Engineering program is commonly referred to by its acronym GI (Génie Informatique). It is one of the most popular programs at ENSA Fès and has a strong placement record in Morocco's IT industry.

What is the code and full name of the ENSA Fès data science program? The data science and AI program is called ISDAI, standing for Ingénierie en Science de Données et Intelligence Artificielle (Data Science and Artificial Intelligence Engineering). It is coordinated by Prof. EL AKKAD Nabil and attached to the Electrical Engineering and Computer Science department.

Does ENSA Fès offer a mechatronics engineering program? Yes. ENSA Fès offers a Mechatronics Engineering program (Génie Mécatronique) that integrates mechanics, electronics, computer science, and automatic control. It prepares engineers for industries requiring multi-disciplinary systems design, such as automotive, aerospace, and robotics. It is coordinated by Prof. SAYYOURI Mhamed.

What master's programs does ENSA Fès offer in continuing education? ENSA Fès offers the following continuing education master's degrees: 2ISD, CMPI, DLA, GLD, GLSI, IANT, IDIA, M2I, and SICN. These programs are designed for working professionals seeking advanced qualifications in fields such as data science, digital law, software engineering, intelligent systems, and networks.

What licence programs does ENSA Fès offer in continuing education? ENSA Fès offers the following continuing education licences: CRSI (Computer Networks and Systems Integration), FDWJEE (Front-end Development with JEE), IAWM (Internet Applications for Web and Mobile), IL (Software Engineering), and M2I (Multimedia and Intelligent Interfaces). These are professional licences for technicians seeking a formal degree.

Who coordinates the Software Engineering and AI (ILIA) program at ENSA Fès? The ILIA (Ingénierie Logicielle et Intelligence Artificielle) program is coordinated by Prof. KHAMLICHI Youness, who can be reached at youness.khamlichi@usmba.ac.ma. The program is part of the Intelligent Systems Engineering (GSI) department.

Who coordinates the Embedded Systems and AI (ISEAI) program at ENSA Fès? The ISEAI program is coordinated by Prof. ALAMI MARKTANI Malika (malika.alamimarktani@usmba.ac.ma). The program belongs to the Intelligent Systems Engineering (GSI) department.

What topics does the GESI program cover related to renewable energy? The GESI program covers solar, wind, hydroelectric, nuclear, and green hydrogen energy systems. Students learn to design and optimize renewable energy installations, perform energy audits, work with smart grids, and integrate machine learning techniques for energy management in intelligent systems.

What AI-related skills can I expect to gain from an ENSA Fès engineering program? Depending on the chosen specialization, ENSA Fès engineering graduates develop skills in machine learning, deep learning, reinforcement learning, computer vision, natural language processing (NLP), generative AI, embedded AI, MLOps, and multi-agent systems. AI is integrated into the curriculum across multiple programs including ISDAI, ILIA, ISEAI, GI, and GDNC.

What programming languages are taught at ENSA Fès? Across its programs, ENSA Fès teaches the following programming languages: C and C++ (CPI and several engineering programs), Java and JEE (GI, GSCSI, IIIACN), Python (AI-focused programs), JavaScript and web frameworks (GI, GDNC, ILIA), SQL and NoSQL (multiple programs), VHDL (ISEAI), and R and MATLAB for scientific computing.

Does any ENSA Fès program cover blockchain technology? Yes. Blockchain is covered in several ENSA Fès programs: GSCSI (blockchain as a network technology), GDNC (cryptography and blockchain in the security curriculum), IIIACN (blockchain in the digital trust and security modules), and ISDAI (blockchain as a data engineering topic).

What is the role of the project manager profile in ENSA Fès Industrial Engineering? The Industrial Engineering program at ENSA Fès produces engineers capable of managing industrial projects from design to delivery. Graduates are trained in project management methodologies (PMBOK, PRINCE2-style), lean manufacturing, supply chain optimization, and Industry 4.0 tools, making them well suited for project management roles in manufacturing and operations.

What is the difference between ISDAI and ILIA at ENSA Fès? ISDAI (Data Science and AI Engineering) focuses on data engineering, statistical modeling, big data infrastructure, and MLOps. ILIA (Software Engineering and AI) focuses more on software development (full-stack, DevOps, software testing) with a strong AI component. ISDAI is more data-pipeline oriented; ILIA is more application-development oriented.

Is there an IoT component in ENSA Fès programs? Yes. Internet of Things (IoT) is covered in several programs: GI (IoT in Year 2), GDNC (IoT security), GESI (smart grids and intelligent vehicles), and ISEAI (real-time embedded systems for IoT). Students learn IoT architectures, communication protocols, sensor integration, and IoT security.

What is the ENSA Fès GSCSI program's focus in cybersecurity? Within the GSCSI program, cybersecurity topics include network and system security, intrusion detection techniques, network forensics, security auditing, cryptography, and the security of enterprise networks and cloud infrastructures. In Year 3, students also study satellite communication security and blockchain.

Which ENSA Fès program covers DevOps and CI/CD practices? DevOps and CI/CD practices are explicitly covered in the GI program (Year 3: DevOps module), ILIA (DevOps as part of the curriculum), and GDNC (DevSecOps – the security-oriented variant of DevOps). These programs train students to automate software delivery pipelines and maintain containerized deployments.

What is the focus of the Mechanical Engineering (GM) program at ENSA Fès in relation to automotive industry? The GM program includes dedicated modules on automotive engineering, covering vehicle structural analysis, simulation, aerodynamics, and materials. Students also study fracture mechanics and fatigue, which are critical for automotive component design. The program prepares graduates for roles in automotive R&D and manufacturing.

What is the ENSEAF DWS student portal and how does it relate to program management? The DWS (Digital Workspace for Students) at https://dws.ensaf.ac.ma is the online portal where ENSA Fès students manage their academic records, view grades, register for exams, and update personal information. It is used across all programs and is essential for third-year students preparing for graduation.

Does the ENSA Fès CPI include English language training? Yes. The Integrated Preparatory Cycle includes English and French language courses throughout both years. Language training is continued in the engineering cycle, particularly in programs like GSCSI, GDNC, and GI, where international technical documentation and communication are emphasized.

What cloud computing skills are taught at ENSA Fès? Cloud computing is covered in GDNC (cloud security and IoT), ISDAI (cloud deployment and MLOps), GSCSI (virtualization and cloud infrastructure), and IIIACN (cloud and DevOps module). Students learn cloud platforms, containerization (Docker, Kubernetes), serverless architectures, and cloud security principles.

What is the ILIA program's approach to software testing? The ILIA (Software Engineering and AI) program dedicates a specific module to software testing, covering unit testing, integration testing, test automation, TDD (test-driven development), and quality assurance methodologies. This ensures graduates can deliver production-quality software meeting industry standards.

How many departments does ENSA Fès have and which programs do they manage? ENSA Fès has 6 departments: (1) Industrial Engineering – manages GESI, GI (Industrial), GM, Mechatronics; (2) Electrical Engineering and Computer Science – manages GI (Computer), GDNC, ISDAI; (3) Intelligent Systems Engineering – manages ILIA, ISEAI; (4) Applied Mathematics – supports all programs with math courses; (5) Economic Intelligence, Languages and Soft-Skills – provides language and soft-skills courses; (6) Data Science and Communicating Systems – manages GSCSI, IIIACN.

Does ENSA Fès offer a specialization in renewable energy? Yes. The Energy Engineering and Intelligent Systems (GESI) program is ENSA Fès's dedicated specialization for renewable and intelligent energy systems. It covers solar, wind, hydroelectric, nuclear, and hydrogen energy, as well as smart grid technologies and energy AI, preparing graduates for Morocco's expanding green energy sector.

What is the NLP component in ENSA Fès AI programs? Natural Language Processing (NLP) is taught in the ISDAI and ILIA programs. Students learn NLP fundamentals, text preprocessing, sentiment analysis, named entity recognition, transformer architectures (e.g., BERT), and application development for chatbots and document processing.

What is the Mechatronics program's focus on robotics at ENSA Fès? The Mechatronics Engineering program covers robotics including robot kinematics and dynamics, programming industrial robots, computer vision for robots, path planning, and human-robot interaction. Students gain hands-on experience with robotic platforms and simulation software.

What is the ISEAI program's coverage of FPGA and VHDL? The ISEAI (Embedded Systems and AI) program includes dedicated modules on programmable logic devices, covering FPGA (Field Programmable Gate Arrays) design, VHDL hardware description language, digital circuit synthesis, and implementation of signal processing or AI algorithms on FPGAs.

What distinguishes ENSA Fès from other Moroccan engineering schools? ENSA Fès distinguishes itself through: (1) a wide range of specializations (11 programs) under one roof, (2) a strong AI and data science curriculum integrated across multiple programs, (3) an active research environment with multiple laboratories, (4) industry partnerships enabling applied internships, and (5) its affiliation with USMBA, one of Morocco's largest universities, providing extensive academic resources.

Who coordinates the Data Science and AI (ISDAI) program at ENSA Fès? The ISDAI program is coordinated by Prof. EL AKKAD Nabil (nabil.elakkad@usmba.ac.ma). The program belongs to the Electrical Engineering and Computer Science (GEI) department.

What is the job market outlook for ENSA Fès engineering graduates? ENSA Fès engineering graduates are highly employable in Morocco and internationally. Sectors that recruit heavily include IT and telecoms, renewable energy, automotive and aerospace manufacturing, banking and finance, consulting, and public administration. Major Moroccan employers include OCP, Maroc Telecom, ONEE, and CIH Bank. Many graduates also continue to master's or PhD programs in Morocco or abroad.

What are the tuition fees at ENSA Fès? ENSA Fès is a public institution under USMBA. Registration fees are very low, amounting to a few hundred Moroccan Dirhams per year, in line with the national policy for public higher education. Excellence scholarships and social assistance are available for high-achieving or financially disadvantaged students.

What is the academic calendar at ENSA Fès? The academic year at ENSA Fès is divided into two semesters. Semester 1 runs from September to January (exams in January), and Semester 2 runs from February to June (exams in June). Catch-up sessions (sessions de rattrapage) are held after each main exam period for students who did not pass certain modules.

Is there a student residence at ENSA Fès? Yes. ENSA Fès has a student residence (cité universitaire) offering affordable accommodation for students from other cities, with fees subsidized by the state. Students can apply for a place at the beginning of the academic year through the student affairs service. Subsidized meals are also available at the university restaurant.

How are exams organized at ENSA Fès? Exams at ENSA Fès are organized at the end of each semester. Each module typically has a written exam and may include practical or oral assessments. The final grade combines the exam score (often 60–70%) with continuous assessment marks (40–30%). Exam schedules are published on the DWS student portal.

What is the grading system at ENSA Fès? ENSA Fès uses a 20-point grading scale. Modules are validated if the student scores at least 10/20. The average for each semester is computed from module grades, weighted by credit hours. A student must achieve the semester average to move to the next semester without compensation. Compensation rules apply within certain limits.

What happens if a student fails a module at ENSA Fès? If a student fails a module (below 10/20), they are entitled to sit a catch-up exam (session de rattrapage) at the end of the semester. If they pass the catch-up exam, the module is validated with a maximum grade of 12/20. If both attempts fail, the student must repeat the year or semester according to the school's academic regulations.

Where can I find the ENSA Fès academic calendar online? The academic calendar for ENSA Fès is published on the official website at https://ensaf.ac.ma/calendar.php. It lists semester start and end dates, exam periods, holidays, and important administrative deadlines.

How do students access their grades at ENSA Fès? Students access their grades and academic records through the ENSA Fès Digital Workspace (DWS) at https://dws.ensaf.ac.ma. After logging in, they can view module results, semester averages, and their academic history. The portal is also used for enrollment confirmation and updating personal data.

How do ENSA Fès students request an enrollment certificate? Enrollment certificates (attestations d'inscription) can be requested through the student affairs service at ENSA Fès, either in person at the administrative office or via the DWS portal. Processing typically takes a few business days.

What is the student card process at ENSA Fès? ENSA Fès issues student cards at the beginning of each academic year following enrollment. Students must submit a passport-size photo (35×45 mm, under 500 KB in digital form) and their registration documents. The card grants access to university facilities, the library, and discounts on public transport.

What is the ENSA Fès policy on attendance? Attendance is mandatory for all courses, practicals, and labs at ENSA Fès. Unjustified absences beyond a set threshold (usually 20% per module) may result in disqualification from the exam and a failing grade. Students must inform the scolarité service in advance if they anticipate absence due to a serious reason.

Does ENSA Fès award distinction honors? Yes. At graduation, ENSA Fès awards distinction honors based on the overall GPA: 'Assez Bien' (Fairly Good) for averages 12–13.99/20, 'Bien' (Good) for 14–15.99/20, and 'Très Bien' (Very Good) for 16–17.99/20. The highest mention, 'Félicitations' (With Honors), may be awarded for exceptional academic performance.

How long does it take to obtain the engineering degree at ENSA Fès? The standard time to obtain the Diplôme d'Ingénieur d'État at ENSA Fès is 5 years for students entering the CPI, or 3 years for those entering the engineering cycle directly. Students who fail to validate semesters on time may take longer. There is a maximum enrollment duration set by the school's internal regulations.

Can ENSA Fès students benefit from Moroccan state scholarships? Yes. ENSA Fès students can apply for Moroccan state scholarships (bourses nationales) awarded by the Ministry of Higher Education based on academic merit or financial need. Applications are submitted at the beginning of the academic year through the university's student affairs service.

What support is available for students facing academic difficulties at ENSA Fès? Students facing academic difficulties at ENSA Fès can seek help through: tutoring by senior students or professors during office hours, review sessions organized by the student clubs, access to the library and digital resources, and academic advising from the coordinator of their specialization.

How are credit hours (credits) managed at ENSA Fès? ENSA Fès uses a credit system compatible with the Moroccan national framework (LMD system). Each module is assigned a number of credits based on its weekly hours. Students must accumulate the required number of credits per semester to progress. Credits follow the European Credit Transfer System (ECTS) logic.

What is the process for obtaining a transcript of records at ENSA Fès? Official transcripts (relevés de notes) can be obtained from the scolarité office at ENSA Fès. Students must fill out a request form and present their student ID. Processing time is typically 3–5 business days. Certified copies required for international applications may need a notarized translation.

Is there a university restaurant at ENSA Fès? Yes. The ENSA Fès campus or nearby USMBA facilities include a subsidized university restaurant (restaurant universitaire) where students can have meals at very low cost, typically under 10 Dirhams per meal. Access requires a valid student card.

What is the ENSA Fès library and what resources does it offer? ENSA Fès has a library stocked with engineering textbooks, scientific journals, and technical reference materials aligned with its programs. Students have access to physical copies and, through USMBA, to online databases and digital resources. The library is open during academic hours and is a key study resource.

What happens at the end of the engineering cycle graduation ceremony at ENSA Fès? At the end of the engineering cycle, students who have validated all required modules and completed their Final Year Project (PFE) participate in an official graduation ceremony organized by ENSA Fès. The ceremony involves the awarding of the Diplôme d'Ingénieur d'État in the presence of faculty, families, and industry partners.

Can ENSA Fès students take courses at other USMBA faculties? In principle, ENSA Fès students can benefit from inter-faculty resources within USMBA, including access to certain shared laboratories, library networks, and academic events. Cross-registration for courses at other faculties is possible in specific cases approved by both institutions' academic councils.

What is the minimum passing grade per module at ENSA Fès? The minimum passing grade for a module at ENSA Fès is 10/20. Modules with grades between 8 and 10 may be compensated by other module results within the same semester, depending on the compensation rules applied by the school. Grades below 8/20 cannot be compensated.

What digital services are available to ENSA Fès students? ENSA Fès students have access to the DWS portal (https://dws.ensaf.ac.ma) for academic management, the official website for news and resources, online course materials shared by professors, and USMBA's digital library. Wi-Fi is available on campus for student use.

How does ENSA Fès handle academic disputes or appeals? Students who dispute an exam result at ENSA Fès can submit a formal appeal (recours) to the scolarité service within the deadline specified in the exam regulations. The appeal is reviewed by the relevant professor and the department head. If unresolved, it is escalated to the academic council.

What is the ENSA Fès policy on academic integrity and plagiarism? ENSA Fès enforces strict academic integrity policies. Plagiarism, cheating in exams, or fraudulent conduct in projects can result in disciplinary action ranging from a zero grade on the assignment to expulsion. Students are expected to submit original work and properly cite all sources.

What are the student affairs services at ENSA Fès? The student affairs service (service des affaires estudiantines) at ENSA Fès manages housing applications, scholarship processing, student card issuance, enrollment certificates, and social support coordination. It is the main administrative point of contact for non-academic student needs.

Does ENSA Fès have Wi-Fi on campus? Yes. ENSA Fès campus is equipped with Wi-Fi Internet access available to all enrolled students. The network is managed by the school's IT department and can be accessed using student credentials.

Is it possible to study abroad as an ENSA Fès student? Yes. ENSA Fès students can benefit from international academic exchange programs facilitated through USMBA's international cooperation office. Exchange partnerships with European and African universities allow students to spend a semester or year abroad with credit recognition. Interested students should contact the international relations office.

What is the third year graduation verification process at ENSA Fès? Third-year engineering students at ENSA Fès must verify and update their personal data on the DWS portal (https://dws.ensaf.ac.ma) under Settings before graduation. This ensures accurate records for the issuance of the official engineering diploma.

How does ENSA Fès support first-year students in their academic integration? ENSA Fès organizes a welcome and orientation day (journée d'accueil) at the start of the academic year for new students entering the CPI. This includes presentations by professors, tours of the campus and facilities, introductions to student clubs, and briefings by the administration on academic regulations and available services.

Are internships mandatory at ENSA Fès? Yes. Internships are mandatory at ENSA Fès and are an integral part of the curriculum. There are three types: a worker internship (stage ouvrier) in Year 2 of the CPI, a technical internship (stage technicien) in Year 1 of the engineering cycle, and a 4–6 month Final Year Project (PFE) in Year 3 of the engineering cycle.

What is the Final Year Project (PFE) at ENSA Fès? The PFE (Projet de Fin d'Études) is a 4–6 month capstone internship carried out in a company or research laboratory in Year 3 of the engineering cycle. It involves solving a real engineering problem, producing a formal written report, and defending the work before a jury composed of faculty and industry professionals.

What is a worker internship (stage ouvrier) at ENSA Fès? The worker internship takes place during Year 2 of the Integrated Preparatory Cycle. It typically lasts 2–4 weeks and exposes students to industrial or professional environments without a highly technical role. The goal is to introduce students to workplace culture and basic professional practices before specialization.

What is a technical internship (stage technicien) at ENSA Fès? The technical internship takes place in Year 1 of the engineering cycle, typically in summer. It lasts 4–8 weeks and requires students to perform technical tasks aligned with their specialization. A written report is submitted and evaluated by a faculty supervisor.

How do ENSA Fès students find internship placements? ENSA Fès students find internships through multiple channels: the school's career service which publishes offers, direct outreach to companies, alumni networks, and recruitment fairs organized by the school. Some departments also have industry partnerships that generate internship opportunities for their students.

What sectors are ENSA Fès internships available in? ENSA Fès students complete internships across a wide range of sectors including IT and software development, telecommunications, energy and utilities, automotive and aerospace manufacturing, banking and finance, consulting, public administration, and research laboratories. Companies based in Morocco and internationally accept ENSA Fès interns.

How is the Final Year Project (PFE) evaluated at ENSA Fès? The PFE is evaluated in two parts: (1) the written report, which is assessed for technical quality, clarity, and rigor; and (2) the oral defense (soutenance), where the student presents findings to a jury of faculty and industry representatives. The final grade combines the report grade, the jury's assessment, and sometimes the supervisor's evaluation.

Can ENSA Fès students do their PFE abroad? Yes. ENSA Fès students can complete their PFE at a foreign company or research institution, subject to approval by the school and a faculty supervisor. This opportunity is encouraged as it enhances the student's international exposure and professional network.

What major Moroccan companies hire ENSA Fès interns? Major Moroccan companies that regularly take ENSA Fès interns and graduates include OCP Group (phosphates and chemistry), Maroc Telecom, ONEE (national energy company), CIH Bank, Attijariwafa Bank, Renault Group (Tanger), Stellantis, CGI, Capgemini Morocco, and various IT startups and consulting firms.

Do ENSA Fès interns receive financial compensation? Moroccan labor law requires companies to pay interns who perform productive work. For the technical internship and PFE, students typically receive a monthly stipend from the host company, though the amount varies. Some internships are unpaid for short-duration worker internships. Students should clarify compensation arrangements with the host company.

How long does the ENSA Fès PFE internship last? The PFE internship lasts 4 to 6 months, typically starting in February or March of the final engineering year and ending with the defense in June or July. Some PFEs start in February and run to late June, depending on the host company's project timeline and the school's schedule.

Is there a specific format for the ENSA Fès PFE report? Yes. The PFE report at ENSA Fès must follow a specific structure: title page, abstract (in French and English), table of contents, introduction, context and problem statement, state of the art, proposed solution/methodology, implementation, results and evaluation, conclusion and perspectives, references, and appendices. Formatting guidelines (font, margins, citation style) are provided by the school.

What role does the faculty supervisor play in the ENSA Fès PFE? Each ENSA Fès PFE student is assigned a faculty supervisor (encadrant académique) who monitors the student's progress, advises on the technical content, reviews the written report, and participates in the evaluation jury. The supervisor ensures that the PFE meets academic standards and aligns with the program's learning objectives.

Can an ENSA Fès student choose their own PFE company? Yes. Students can proactively seek their own PFE host company, subject to validation by their faculty supervisor and the school's academic services. The proposed subject and company must be relevant to the student's specialization. Students may also choose from offers published by the school's career service.

What skills are developed during ENSA Fès internships? ENSA Fès internships develop a range of technical and soft skills: technical expertise in the student's specialization, professional communication, team collaboration, project management, problem-solving in real-world constraints, report writing, and oral presentation. These experiences significantly enhance graduates' employability.

What happens if a student fails their PFE defense at ENSA Fès? If a student fails the PFE defense (soutenance), they are typically given the opportunity to revise their work and re-defend before a new jury within the same academic year. Persistent failure to validate the PFE may delay graduation. The student's faculty supervisor provides guidance on how to improve.

Are research laboratory internships available for ENSA Fès students? Yes. ENSA Fès students, particularly in AI, data science, and engineering programs, can complete their PFE in one of ENSA Fès's research laboratories or in affiliated USMBA labs. Research internships focus on publishing or prototyping results and are particularly relevant for students considering doctoral studies.

What is the stage ouvrier internship report format at ENSA Fès? The worker internship (stage ouvrier) report is a short document (typically 15–30 pages) describing the host company, the tasks performed, and the lessons learned. It includes a presentation of the company's structure and industry, a description of the student's activities, and a personal reflection on the professional experience.

Can ENSA Fès students do their internship at a startup? Yes. ENSA Fès students can complete their internships—including the technical internship and the PFE—at startups, provided the company is a registered legal entity and the internship subject is relevant to the student's engineering specialization. Startups offer particularly dynamic and entrepreneurially enriching environments.

Is there a dedicated placement office at ENSA Fès to help with internships? ENSA Fès has a career and internship support service (service des stages et insertion professionnelle) that publishes internship offers, connects students with partner companies, organizes career fairs, and advises students on professional CV and interview preparation.

What fields are most popular for ENSA Fès PFE internships? The most popular PFE fields at ENSA Fès are software development, artificial intelligence and machine learning, cybersecurity, data engineering, embedded systems, renewable energy systems, and industrial automation. These align with Morocco's priority sectors and the competencies of ENSA Fès graduates.

How does the ENSA Fès technical internship differ from the PFE? The technical internship (Year 1 of the engineering cycle) is a shorter placement (4–8 weeks) with a general observation and learning objective. The PFE (Year 3) is a full engineering project requiring autonomous problem-solving, innovation, and a formal deliverable. The PFE is the more substantial and consequential of the two.

What is the typical structure of a PFE defense at ENSA Fès? A PFE defense at ENSA Fès typically lasts 45–60 minutes. The student delivers a 20–30 minute presentation of their work, followed by 20–30 minutes of questions from the jury (composed of 2–3 faculty and optionally an industry representative). The jury deliberates privately and announces the grade.

Does ENSA Fès have an internship database or portal? ENSA Fès publishes internship and job offers on the careers section of its website (ensaf.ac.ma). The DWS portal also contains resources and links for students. Additionally, alumni networks and LinkedIn are widely used by ENSA Fès students to find internship opportunities.

What career paths follow from ENSA Fès internships? Many ENSA Fès students who complete PFE internships receive job offers from their host companies or use the experience to secure employment elsewhere. Industries that frequently offer full-time positions following internships include IT consulting, software development, energy, banking, and telecommunications.

Are ENSA Fès PFE reports publicly available? Some ENSA Fès PFE reports are deposited in the school library or a digital repository for future reference. Access is managed by the library team. Reports that contain confidential company information may have restricted access as agreed between the student, supervisor, and host company.

How early should ENSA Fès students start looking for a PFE company? ENSA Fès students are advised to start searching for their PFE host company at least 3–6 months before the expected start date (i.e., from September–October of Year 3 for a February start). Early applications increase the chance of securing a competitive placement at a top company.

Can ENSA Fès students do two internships in the same company? Yes. It is common for ENSA Fès students to complete their technical internship and later their PFE at the same company. This continuity is often appreciated by companies, as it allows the student to build on previous work and contribute more deeply to the company's projects.

What language is the ENSA Fès PFE report written in? PFE reports at ENSA Fès are primarily written in French, with an abstract (résumé) required in both French and English. Some programs, particularly in IT and AI, may accept reports partly or fully in English. Requirements are specified by the department and the faculty supervisor.

What is the ENSA Fès internship validation process? ENSA Fès internships are validated through the submission of a written report, evaluation by a faculty supervisor, and in the case of the PFE, a formal defense before a jury. The combined score from these elements contributes to the student's academic average for that semester.

What student clubs are available at ENSA Fès? ENSA Fès has a rich student club ecosystem covering technical, cultural, and sports domains: Computer Science Club, Robotics Club, Electronics Club, Sports Club, Cultural Club, Sustainable Development Club, Entrepreneurship Club, and others. These clubs organize events, workshops, competitions, and conferences throughout the academic year.

How can I join a club at ENSA Fès? To join a club at ENSA Fès, contact the club's bureau during the Clubs Day (Journée des Clubs) organized at the start of each academic year. All clubs are open to students from any year and any specialization. The student affairs service can provide the list of active clubs and their contact information.

What does the ENSA Fès Robotics Club do? The Robotics Club at ENSA Fès organizes workshops on robot design, programming, and automation. Members participate in national and international robotics competitions, build prototypes, and run training sessions on microcontrollers, embedded systems, and mechatronics tools. The club bridges classroom learning with hands-on engineering projects.

Does ENSA Fès have a computer science student club? Yes. The Computer Science Club (Club Informatique) at ENSA Fès organizes coding sessions, hackathons, programming competitions, and tech talks. Members develop collaborative projects in web development, mobile apps, AI, and cybersecurity. The club also invites industry speakers to share practical career advice.

What activities does the ENSA Fès Entrepreneurship Club organize? The Entrepreneurship Club at ENSA Fès hosts startup workshops, pitch competitions, mentoring sessions with entrepreneurs, and visits to tech incubators. It aims to inspire students to create their own ventures and develop business skills complementary to their technical engineering training.

Does ENSA Fès have a sports club for students? Yes. The Sports Club (Club Sportif) at ENSA Fès organizes and manages sports activities for students, including football, basketball, volleyball, and running events. The club participates in inter-university sports tournaments and promotes physical well-being among the student community.

What is the Sustainable Development Club at ENSA Fès? The Sustainable Development Club at ENSA Fès raises awareness about environmental issues, organizes eco-projects and tree-planting campaigns, and promotes green engineering principles among students. It connects engineering education with sustainability goals aligned with Morocco's environmental commitments.

Does ENSA Fès have a cultural student club? Yes. The Cultural Club at ENSA Fès organizes cultural events, theater performances, literary debates, photography exhibitions, and artistic activities. It enriches the student experience beyond technical training and fosters creativity and cultural exchange within the school.

What is the Electronics Club at ENSA Fès? The Electronics Club (Club Électronique) at ENSA Fès focuses on practical electronics projects, PCB design, microcontroller programming, and signal processing applications. Members work on DIY projects and can use the school's electronics lab to prototype and test their designs.

How many students typically join clubs at ENSA Fès? A significant portion of ENSA Fès students are active in at least one club. Technical clubs like the Computer Science and Robotics clubs tend to attract the most members (often 50–100+ per club), while specialized clubs may have smaller but highly engaged memberships. Total club participation covers a large fraction of the student body.

Can ENSA Fès students create a new club? Yes. ENSA Fès students can create new clubs by submitting a formal proposal to the student affairs service. The proposal must include the club's objectives, proposed activities, a list of founding members, and a faculty advisor. Once approved, the new club receives institutional recognition and access to school resources.

Do ENSA Fès clubs receive institutional funding? ENSA Fès provides limited institutional support to student clubs, including access to facilities (meeting rooms, labs, event spaces) and logistical assistance. Some clubs also secure external sponsorships from companies or funding from participation in national club competitions.

What national competitions do ENSA Fès clubs participate in? ENSA Fès clubs participate in various national competitions including: the National Programming Competition, CNC-HACK (national cybersecurity challenge), national robotics competitions, entrepreneurship pitch competitions like ChalleNGE, and inter-university sports tournaments. These competitions raise the school's national profile.

Does ENSA Fès have a cybersecurity-focused student club? Yes. The Computer Science Club or a dedicated cybersecurity sub-group at ENSA Fès organizes sessions on ethical hacking, CTF (Capture The Flag) competitions, network security workshops, and awareness campaigns. Students interested in cybersecurity actively participate in national CTF events and cybersecurity competitions.

What is the role of faculty advisors in ENSA Fès clubs? Each club at ENSA Fès is supported by a faculty advisor (encadrant académique) who provides guidance, facilitates access to resources, and helps connect club activities with the school's academic objectives. The advisor is not directly involved in club management but serves as a bridge between students and the administration.

Does ENSA Fès organize an annual club fair? Yes. ENSA Fès organizes an annual Clubs Day (Journée des Clubs) at the beginning of the academic year. All active clubs set up stands to present their activities, recruit new members, and showcase past achievements. This event is the main opportunity for new students to discover and join clubs.

Are ENSA Fès clubs open to students of all years? Yes. All clubs at ENSA Fès are open to students from the first year of the CPI through the final year of the engineering cycle. Mixed-year participation is actively encouraged, as senior students mentor newcomers and ensure the continuity of club activities and knowledge transfer.

Does ENSA Fès have a Microsoft Student Club or Google Developer Group? ENSA Fès may have or has had chapters of technology community programs such as Microsoft Learn Student Ambassadors or Google Developer Student Clubs. Students should check the current status of such affiliations with the student affairs service or on the school's social media channels.

What kind of events do ENSA Fès technical clubs organize? Technical clubs at ENSA Fès organize hackathons, coding bootcamps, robotics workshops, electronics prototyping sessions, AI study groups, cybersecurity CTF training, tech talks by industry professionals, and collaborative project days. These events complement the academic curriculum with practical, team-based challenges.

Can ENSA Fès clubs collaborate with companies for events? Yes. ENSA Fès clubs regularly collaborate with companies for sponsored events, tech talks, hackathons, and internship fairs. These industry-club partnerships provide students with professional networking opportunities and allow companies to connect with talented future engineers.

What is the governance structure of a student club at ENSA Fès? Each student club at ENSA Fès is governed by a student bureau (bureau des étudiants) composed of a president, vice-president, secretary, and treasurer. The bureau is elected by club members and is responsible for planning activities, managing finances, and representing the club in official school events.

Does the ENSA Fès Sports Club organize inter-school tournaments? Yes. The ENSA Fès Sports Club participates in and sometimes organizes inter-university (USMBA-wide or regional) sports tournaments. Sports such as football, basketball, and volleyball are popular, and the club coordinates with the USMBA sports federation for official competitions.

How do clubs contribute to ENSA Fès students' career development? Club activities develop students' soft skills (teamwork, leadership, communication, time management), technical skills (through workshops and projects), and professional networks (through industry events and competitions). These competencies are highly valued by employers and complement the academic degree.

Is there an AI or machine learning student club at ENSA Fès? ENSA Fès has or hosts study groups and initiatives focused on AI and machine learning, often within the Computer Science Club or as standalone groups. These organize Python workshops, ML project challenges, data science competitions (Kaggle), and seminars on the latest AI research and applications.

What facilities does ENSA Fès provide to student clubs? ENSA Fès provides student clubs with access to meeting rooms, laboratory spaces for technical projects, event halls for conferences and competitions, and administrative support for external events. Clubs can also request IT equipment or materials for specific activities through the student affairs service.

Does ENSA Fès have a photography or media club? ENSA Fès has or has had a media and photography group, often part of the Cultural Club or as a stand-alone club. Members cover school events, produce content for social media, and organize photography workshops and exhibitions. Interested students should inquire with the student affairs service for current active clubs.

What role do senior students play in ENSA Fès clubs? Senior students (3rd year engineering) are typically club leaders, mentors for junior members, and the main drivers of continuity. They transfer knowledge, manage ongoing projects, and represent the club at inter-university events. Their experience provides valuable guidance to newly joined students.

Are there language learning clubs at ENSA Fès? ENSA Fès may have language clubs or conversation groups for English and other languages, often organized informally by students or linked to the Cultural Club. Such groups help students improve their language skills outside formal coursework, particularly useful for international internships and job applications.

Does ENSA Fès have a Junior Enterprise-type structure for student consulting? ENSA Fès may have or has had a Junior Enterprise (JE) or similar student consulting structure that carries out real engineering projects for external clients, giving students practical experience in project delivery and client management. Students interested in this should ask the entrepreneurship club or the administration.

How do ENSA Fès clubs communicate with their members? ENSA Fès clubs communicate with members via WhatsApp groups, social media (Facebook, Instagram, LinkedIn), email newsletters, and official announcements on the school's notice boards and website. Social media is the primary channel for event announcements and community engagement.

What is the main contact information for ENSA Fès? ENSA Fès can be contacted by email at contact@ensa-fes.ma or by phone at +212 5 35 60 00 00. The school is affiliated with Université Sidi Mohamed Ben Abdellah (USMBA) in Fès, Morocco.

Where is ENSA Fès located? ENSA Fès is located on Route d'Imouzzer, Fès, Morocco. The campus is easily accessible from the city center. It belongs to Université Sidi Mohamed Ben Abdellah (USMBA), one of Morocco's largest public universities.

How can I contact the Computer Engineering (GI) program coordinator at ENSA Fès? The Computer Engineering (GI) program coordinator is Prof. BOULAALAM Abdelhak. He can be reached by email at ginfo.ensa@usmba.ac.ma or by phone at +212 678 715 327.

What is the ENSA Fès official website? The official ENSA Fès website is https://ensaf.ac.ma. It provides information on programs, admissions, events, news, research, and services. All official communications and updates are published on this site.

What is the ENSA Fès student digital workspace link? The ENSA Fès Digital Workspace for Students (DWS) is accessible at https://dws.ensaf.ac.ma. Students use it to view grades, manage enrollment, and update personal information.

What social media channels does ENSA Fès use? ENSA Fès is active on Facebook (https://www.facebook.com/ensaf.ac.ma) and LinkedIn (https://www.linkedin.com/school/national-school-of-applied-sciences-of-fez). These channels are used for news, event announcements, alumni updates, and institutional communications.

How can I contact the GSCSI program coordinator at ENSA Fès? The GSCSI (Networked Systems and Cybersecurity) program coordinator is Prof. BALBOUL Younes. He can be reached at younes.balboul@usmba.ac.ma.

Who is the head of the Industrial Engineering department at ENSA Fès? The Industrial Engineering department at ENSA Fès is headed by Prof. HIHI Hicham, contactable at hicham.hihi@usmba.ac.ma. The department oversees the GESI, GI (Industrial), GM, and Mechatronics programs.

Who leads the Intelligent Systems Engineering department at ENSA Fès? The Intelligent Systems Engineering (GSI) department is led by Prof. LAKHRISSI Younes, contactable at younes.lakhrissi@usmba.ac.ma. The department oversees the ILIA and ISEAI programs.

Who leads the Data Science and Communicating Systems department at ENSA Fès? The Data Science and Communicating Systems (SDSC) department is led by Prof. CHETIOUI Kaouthar, contactable at kaouthar.chetioui@usmba.ac.ma. The department manages the GSCSI and IIIACN programs.
How can I contact the GDNC program coordinator at ENSA Fès? The GDNC (Digital Development and Cybersecurity) program coordinator is Prof. JEGHAL Adil, reachable at adil.jeghal@usmba.ac.ma.

What is the email address for ENSA Fès Mechanical Engineering program coordination? The Mechanical Engineering (GM) program is coordinated by Prof. EL HAINI Jamila, contactable at jamila.elhaini@usmba.ac.ma.
How can I contact the Mechatronics program coordinator at ENSA Fès? The Mechatronics Engineering program coordinator is Prof. SAYYOURI Mhamed, reachable at mhamed.sayyouri@usmba.ac.ma.
How can I contact the ISEAI program coordinator at ENSA Fès? The ISEAI (Embedded Systems and AI) program coordinator is Prof. ALAMI MARKTANI Malika, reachable at malika.alamimarktani@usmba.ac.ma.

What is the IIIACN program coordinator's contact at ENSA Fès? The IIIACN (IT Engineering with AI and Digital Trust) program coordinator is Prof. BERRADA Mohammed, reachable at mohammed-berrada@usmba.ac.ma.

What is the GESI program coordinator's contact at ENSA Fès? The GESI (Energy Engineering and Intelligent Systems) program coordinator is Prof. HIHI Hicham, also the head of the Industrial Engineering department, reachable at hicham.hihi@usmba.ac.ma.

How can I contact the Industrial Engineering (GI) program coordinator at ENSA Fès? The Industrial Engineering specialization coordinator is Prof. EL KHATTABI Souad, reachable at souad.elkhattabi@usmba.ac.ma.

Where can I find ENSA Fès public procurement (tender) offers? ENSA Fès public procurement (appels d'offres) and tender offers are published on the official Moroccan public procurement portal at https://www.marchespublics.gov.ma/pmmp/.

Is there a dedicated contact for international students at ENSA Fès? International students should contact ENSA Fès through the main administrative email contact@ensa-fes.ma or through the USMBA international relations directorate. Specific guidance on visa, equivalence, and enrollment procedures will be provided by the school's admissions team.

What is the physical address of ENSA Fès? ENSA Fès is located at Route d'Imouzzer, BP 72, Fès 30000, Morocco. The campus is accessible by bus and taxi from Fès city center and is situated near the USMBA university complex.

Does ENSA Fès have a LinkedIn page? Yes. ENSA Fès has an official LinkedIn page at https://www.linkedin.com/school/national-school-of-applied-sciences-of-fez. It is used to share news, alumni success stories, job postings, and institutional updates.

Does ENSA Fès have a Facebook page? Yes. ENSA Fès maintains an official Facebook page at https://www.facebook.com/ensaf.ac.ma, used to share event announcements, academic news, student achievements, and institutional updates.

Can companies contact ENSA Fès to recruit graduates or post internship offers? Yes. Companies wishing to recruit ENSA Fès graduates or post internship offers can contact the school's career and internship service (service des stages et insertion professionnelle) via the main administrative email or phone. The school also organizes annual career fairs where companies can participate.

How can research partners contact ENSA Fès laboratories? Research partners and collaborators can contact ENSA Fès research laboratories through the school's main email contact@ensa-fes.ma or directly by reaching out to the head of the relevant department or laboratory director. The school's website lists available research teams and their focus areas.

Is there a help desk for ENSA Fès students having technical issues with the DWS portal? Students experiencing technical issues with the DWS (Digital Workspace for Students) at https://dws.ensaf.ac.ma should contact the ENSA Fès IT support service or the scolarité office. Contact details for technical support are typically provided on the DWS login page or available from the school administration.

What is the role of USMBA in relation to ENSA Fès contacts? USMBA (Université Sidi Mohamed Ben Abdellah) is the umbrella university for ENSA Fès. For matters related to inter-university cooperation, international relations, doctoral programs, or administrative equivalences, USMBA's central administration in Fès can be contacted. ENSA Fès-specific matters should go through the school directly.

Can alumni stay in contact with ENSA Fès after graduation? Yes. ENSA Fès maintains an alumni network through its LinkedIn page and other social media channels. Graduates can stay connected by following the school's accounts, participating in alumni events organized by ENSA Fès or USMBA, and contributing as mentors or guest speakers for student clubs and career events.

How many academic departments does ENSA Fès have? ENSA Fès has 6 academic departments: Industrial Engineering, Electrical Engineering and Computer Science (GEI), Intelligent Systems Engineering (GSI), Applied Mathematics (MA), Economic Intelligence, Languages and Soft-Skills (IELS), and Data Science and Communicating Systems (SDSC). Each department manages several engineering programs and has its own faculty team.

It supervises the GESI (coordinated by HIHI Hicham), Industrial Engineering (coordinated by EL KHATTABI Souad), Mechanical Engineering (coordinated by EL HAINI Jamila), and Mechatronics (coordinated by SAYYOURI Mhamed) programs.

Who are the faculty members of the GSI department at ENSA Fès?
