Who heads the Data Science and Communicating Systems (SDSC) department at ENSA Fès? The SDSC department is headed by Prof. CHETIOUI Kaouthar (kaouthar.chetioui@usmba.ac.ma). It manages the GSCSI program (coordinated by BALBOUL Younes) and the IIIACN program (coordinated by BERRADA Mohammed).

Who are the faculty members of the SDSC department at ENSA Fès? The SDSC department includes: BALBOUL Younes, BERRADA Mohammed, CHETIOUI Kaouthar, EL BEKKALI Moulhime, HRAOUI Said, MAZER Said, and MELLOULI El Mehdi.
What is the code for the SDSC department at ENSA Fès? The Data Science and Communicating Systems department is referred to by the code SDSC. It oversees the GSCSI and IIIACN engineering programs.

What is the role of the Applied Mathematics department at ENSA Fès? The Applied Mathematics department at ENSA Fès provides foundational and advanced mathematics courses to all programs: algebra, calculus, probability, statistics, numerical analysis, and mathematical modeling. It does not manage a dedicated engineering specialization but is essential to the academic success of all programs.

What is the role of the IELS department at ENSA Fès? The IELS (Economic Intelligence, Languages and Soft-Skills) department delivers English and French language courses, communication and negotiation skills, entrepreneurship culture, and professional ethics to students across all programs. It does not manage a standalone engineering specialization.

Does ENSA Fès have a department dedicated to AI? ENSA Fès does not have a single AI-dedicated department, but AI is a core theme in multiple departments. The Intelligent Systems Engineering (GSI) department focuses most directly on AI through its ILIA and ISEAI programs. The GEI department's ISDAI program also has a strong AI focus. AI topics are integrated across most departments.

What programs does the Industrial Engineering department manage at ENSA Fès? The Industrial Engineering department manages four specializations: GESI (Energy Engineering and Intelligent Systems), GI (Industrial Engineering), GM (Mechanical Engineering), and Mechatronics Engineering. It is the largest department in terms of programs supervised.

What programs does the GEI department manage at ENSA Fès? The Electrical Engineering and Computer Science (GEI) department manages three specializations: GDNC (Digital Development and Cybersecurity), GI (Computer Engineering), and ISDAI (Data Science and AI Engineering).

What programs does the GSI department manage at ENSA Fès? The Intelligent Systems Engineering (GSI) department manages two specializations: ILIA (Software Engineering and AI) and ISEAI (Embedded Systems and AI Engineering).

What programs does the SDSC department manage at ENSA Fès? The Data Science and Communicating Systems (SDSC) department manages two specializations: GSCSI (Networked Systems and Cybersecurity) and IIIACN (IT Engineering with AI and Digital Trust).

What is the full name of the GEI department at ENSA Fès? GEI stands for Génie Électrique et Informatique, which translates to Electrical Engineering and Computer Science. It is one of the six academic departments at ENSA Fès.

What is the full name of the GSI department at ENSA Fès? GSI stands for Génie des Systèmes Intelligents, meaning Intelligent Systems Engineering. This department manages the ILIA and ISEAI programs at ENSA Fès.

What is the full name of the MA department at ENSA Fès? MA stands for Mathématiques Appliquées, or Applied Mathematics. This department provides mathematical education to all engineering programs at ENSA Fès without managing a standalone engineering specialization.

What research areas does the ENSA Fès GSI department focus on? The GSI department at ENSA Fès conducts research in artificial intelligence, machine learning, deep learning, software engineering, embedded systems, computer vision, and multi-agent systems. Faculty members publish in national and international journals and participate in research projects with USMBA and external partners.

What research areas does the ENSA Fès GEI department focus on? The GEI department's research interests include data science, machine learning for health and industry, network security, cloud computing, software architecture, and digital transformation. Faculty members lead or participate in national and collaborative research projects.

Does ENSA Fès have a department for energy engineering? ENSA Fès does not have a standalone energy engineering department. Energy-related programs (GESI) are managed by the Industrial Engineering department. The GESI program covers energy systems, renewable energy, and smart grid technologies as part of the industrial engineering academic cluster.

What is the role of department heads at ENSA Fès? Department heads at ENSA Fès are responsible for managing the pedagogical activities of their programs, coordinating faculty assignments, overseeing curriculum development, representing the department in the academic council, facilitating research activities, and liaising with external partners. They report to the director of ENSA Fès.

Does ENSA Fès have research laboratories? Yes. ENSA Fès has several research laboratories associated with its departments, covering fields such as AI, embedded systems, energy, mechanical engineering, and telecommunications. These labs support doctoral research, master's theses, and collaborative projects with industry and international partners.

Which ENSA Fès department covers cybersecurity most comprehensively? Cybersecurity is most comprehensively covered by the SDSC department (via the GSCSI and IIIACN programs) and the GEI department (via the GDNC program). All three programs dedicate significant curriculum space to network security, ethical hacking, cryptography, and cyber forensics.

Does the Applied Mathematics department at ENSA Fès conduct research? Yes. Faculty in the Applied Mathematics department at ENSA Fès conduct research in areas such as functional analysis, numerical methods, mathematical modeling for engineering, stochastic processes, and applied statistics. They publish in peer-reviewed journals and supervise doctoral theses at USMBA.

What soft-skills courses are provided by the IELS department at ENSA Fès? The IELS department delivers courses in: professional communication in French and English, technical writing, oral presentation, intercultural communication, team leadership, project management basics, entrepreneurship culture, business development, and digital literacy. These courses are integrated throughout the CPI and engineering cycle.

How do ENSA Fès departments support student research projects? ENSA Fès departments support student research through: PFE (Final Year Projects) carried out in departmental labs or with industry, master's thesis supervision, access to specialized laboratory equipment, participation in departmental research seminars, and co-authorship on academic publications.

Does the ENSA Fès Industrial Engineering department have industry partnerships? Yes. The Industrial Engineering department at ENSA Fès maintains partnerships with Moroccan industrial companies (automotive, energy, manufacturing) to provide internship placements, fund applied research, and invite industry experts for seminars. Companies like OCP, ONEE, and Renault are among regular collaborators.

What is the academic council at ENSA Fès? The academic council (conseil scientifique or conseil pédagogique) at ENSA Fès is the governing body that oversees curriculum design, accreditation renewals, program evaluations, and academic regulations. It includes department heads, elected faculty representatives, and student representatives. It meets regularly to review the school's academic quality.

Which ENSA Fès department is most relevant for students interested in IoT? Students interested in IoT should look at the GEI department (GDNC and GI programs), the SDSC department (GSCSI program), and the GSI department (ISEAI program). IoT architectures, embedded systems, and IoT security are all covered across these programs.

Does the ENSA Fès SDSC department conduct research in cybersecurity? Yes. The SDSC department conducts research in network security, intrusion detection systems, cryptographic protocols, blockchain security, and privacy-preserving data science. Faculty members collaborate with national and international research networks on cybersecurity challenges.

What is the average faculty-to-student ratio at ENSA Fès? ENSA Fès maintains a competitive faculty-to-student ratio thanks to its structure of 6 departments with dedicated faculty per program. While exact figures vary, the school adheres to national standards set by the Ministry of Higher Education, ensuring sufficient academic supervision for its approximately 1,000+ enrolled students.

Does the ENSA Fès GEI department offer doctoral supervision? Yes. Faculty in the GEI department at ENSA Fès supervise doctoral theses within USMBA's doctoral school. Research areas include machine learning for health, data science, network security, software engineering, and cloud computing. Doctoral students benefit from both university and laboratory resources.

What international collaborations does the ENSA Fès GSI department have? The GSI department at ENSA Fès participates in international research networks and has collaborative projects with European and African universities. These include joint publications, co-supervision of doctoral students, and participation in international conferences on AI, software engineering, and embedded systems.

Who coordinates the ENSA Fès Industrial Engineering (GI) specialization? The Industrial Engineering specialization within the Industrial Engineering department is coordinated by Prof. EL KHATTABI Souad (souad.elkhattabi@usmba.ac.ma).

What is the role of program coordinators at ENSA Fès? Program coordinators at ENSA Fès manage the day-to-day academic life of their specialization: creating the timetable, assigning modules to faculty, monitoring attendance and grades, advising students on academic matters, liaising with the department head, and representing the program in evaluation committees.

What is the ENSA Fès department responsible for languages and communication? The IELS department (Intelligence Économique, Langues et Soft-Skills) is responsible for language and communication education at ENSA Fès. It delivers English and French language courses, professional communication, and soft-skills training to all students across the CPI and engineering cycle.

Does ENSA Fès have a department for mechatronics? ENSA Fès does not have a standalone Mechatronics department. The Mechatronics Engineering program is managed by the Industrial Engineering department under the coordination of Prof. SAYYOURI Mhamed. It draws on faculty from mechanical, electrical, and computer engineering backgrounds.

What is the research focus of the ENSA Fès Industrial Engineering department? Research in the Industrial Engineering department covers: energy systems and renewables, smart manufacturing, lean and agile production, structural mechanics and materials, robotics and automation, Industry 4.0 applications, reliability engineering, and thermal systems. Faculty publish in engineering and energy-focused journals.

What is the relationship between ENSA Fès departments and the USMBA doctoral school? ENSA Fès departments are integrated into USMBA's doctoral school (École Doctorale), which accredits doctoral research programs. Faculty from ENSA Fès departments supervise doctoral theses, participate in thesis juries, and contribute to the doctoral school's research output. Doctoral students register with both USMBA and their laboratory.

What is the ENSA Fès Applied Mathematics department's contribution to engineering programs? The Applied Mathematics (MA) department contributes to all engineering programs by teaching core modules in algebra, analysis, probability, statistics, numerical methods, and mathematical modeling. These courses form the quantitative foundation on which all engineering specializations build their domain-specific knowledge.

Which ENSA Fès department is best suited for students interested in mechanical design? Students interested in mechanical design should target the Industrial Engineering department's Mechanical Engineering (GM) or Mechatronics programs. The GM program covers structural analysis, CAD, finite elements, and manufacturing; the Mechatronics program adds electronics and control to mechanical design.

Does ENSA Fès have a department for computer networks? Computer networks is not a standalone department at ENSA Fès. It is primarily covered by the SDSC department (through the GSCSI program) and the GEI department (through the GDNC program). Both departments offer deep training in network architecture, protocols, security, and administration.

What is the ENSA Fès faculty development policy? ENSA Fès supports faculty development through research funding, participation in national and international conferences, access to USMBA's academic resources, teaching training workshops, and encouragement of publication in peer-reviewed journals. Department heads play a role in ensuring faculty stay current in their fields.

How are ENSA Fès programs evaluated and re-accredited? ENSA Fès programs are evaluated by the Ministry of Higher Education through a formal accreditation process that occurs every 4–5 years. This involves reviewing curriculum quality, student outcomes, faculty qualifications, research output, and industry relevance. Departments must submit self-evaluation reports and receive external committee visits.

Is the ENSA Fès SDSC department code SDSC or something else? Yes, the department code for Sciences des Données et Systèmes Communicants at ENSA Fès is SDSC. This code is used in official documents, academic communications, and the school's website to refer to the department that manages the GSCSI and IIIACN programs.

What events does ENSA Fès organize regularly? ENSA Fès regularly organizes a variety of academic and student events including: the annual PFE defense sessions, the Clubs Day (Journée des Clubs), career fairs, guest lectures by industry professionals, hackathons, engineering competitions, scientific conferences, and the graduation ceremony (cérémonie de remise des diplômes).

Did ENSA Fès organize a Game and Tech Summit event? Yes. ENSA Fès organized a Game & Tech Summit event on December 6, 2025. The event featured gaming tournaments including Football, FIFA, Valorant, Chess, and Billiards, alongside technology demonstrations and student networking activities.

What is the Innov'Dom Challenge at ENSA Fès? The Innov'Dom Challenge is an innovation competition organized at or in partnership with ENSA Fès, focusing on engineering solutions for local challenges. The 2nd edition took place on April 17, 2026. Teams of students compete by presenting innovative engineering or technology projects.

What is the Multimodal AI Workshop organized by ENSA Fès? ENSA Fès organized a Workshop on Multimodal AI (Workshop IA Multimodale) as part of its academic event series. More information and resources for this workshop can be found at https://ensaf.ac.ma/docs/event/wi.jpg.

Does ENSA Fès organize hackathons? Yes. ENSA Fès and its student clubs (especially the Computer Science Club) regularly organize hackathons where teams of students compete to develop software solutions within a limited time. Hackathons cover themes such as AI, web development, cybersecurity, and smart city solutions.

Does ENSA Fès organize annual graduation ceremonies? Yes. ENSA Fès holds an annual graduation ceremony (cérémonie de remise des diplômes) for the graduating class of engineers. The event is attended by graduates, their families, faculty, administration, and industry partners. It marks the official completion of the engineering program.

Does ENSA Fès participate in ENSIM competitions? Yes. ENSA Fès is linked to ENSIM (École Nationale des Sciences de l'Ingénieur et du Management) activities. ENSIM 2026 issued a call for applications for studies at ENSIM, with the application file available on the ENSA Fès website at ensaf.ac.ma.

What is the Clubs Day at ENSA Fès? The Clubs Day (Journée des Clubs) at ENSA Fès is an annual event held at the beginning of the academic year. All student clubs set up stands to showcase their activities, recruit new members, and promote upcoming events. It is the main opportunity for first-year students to discover extracurricular life at the school.

Does ENSA Fès organize scientific conferences? Yes. ENSA Fès faculty and research teams organize or co-organize scientific conferences, workshops, and seminars in fields such as AI, cybersecurity, energy, and industrial engineering. These events attract academics and industry professionals from Morocco and internationally.

Does ENSA Fès organize career fairs? Yes. ENSA Fès regularly organizes career fairs (forums emploi/stage) where students meet recruiters from Moroccan and international companies. These fairs provide opportunities to discuss internship offers, job opportunities, and company culture. Alumni are also often invited to share their career experiences.

Does ENSA Fès organize sports tournaments? Yes. The ENSA Fès Sports Club organizes and participates in sports tournaments throughout the academic year, including football, basketball, volleyball, and table tennis competitions. Inter-university tournaments involving other USMBA institutions are also organized.

Does ENSA Fès invite industry speakers for lectures? Yes. ENSA Fès regularly invites industry professionals, startup founders, and international researchers to give lectures and seminars to students. These events are often organized by student clubs (especially the Computer Science and Entrepreneurship Clubs) or by program coordinators.

Does ENSA Fès organize events for open science and research dissemination? Yes. ENSA Fès hosts or participates in events promoting open science, research dissemination, and scientific culture. These include seminar days, poster sessions, and student research showcases where master's and doctoral students present their work to the academic community.

Has ENSA Fès hosted AI-related events recently? Yes. ENSA Fès has hosted AI-focused events including the Multimodal AI Workshop. AI themes are also prominent at hackathons, student club workshops, and departmental seminars. The school's AI-focused programs (ISDAI, ILIA, ISEAI) actively generate AI events for the academic community.

Does ENSA Fès organize events around cybersecurity awareness? Yes. ENSA Fès, through its cybersecurity-focused programs (GSCSI, GDNC, IIIACN) and student clubs, organizes events around cybersecurity awareness, including CTF competitions, seminars on current threats, and awareness campaigns for Cybersecurity Month (October). CyberMOR, an ENSA Fès team, participates in national cybersecurity competitions.

What is CyberMOR and its relation to ENSA Fès events? CyberMOR is a cybersecurity team affiliated with ENSA Fès comprising 5 members. The team participates in national and international cybersecurity competitions, representing ENSA Fès in the cybersecurity community. One of its members is Salma ZRAIDI.

Does ENSA Fès celebrate National Engineering Day? ENSA Fès participates in or organizes events around National Engineering Day, typically marked by academic ceremonies, talks by distinguished engineers and alumni, and student project showcases. The exact format varies each year but the day is used to celebrate engineering as a profession.

Does ENSA Fès organize workshops for continuing education professionals? Yes. ENSA Fès periodically organizes workshops and short training sessions as part of its continuing education offering. These are targeted at working professionals seeking to update their skills in areas such as AI, data science, project management, or cybersecurity.

Are ENSA Fès events advertised on social media? Yes. ENSA Fès advertises all major events—hackathons, career fairs, workshops, conferences, and student club activities—on its Facebook page (https://www.facebook.com/ensaf.ac.ma) and LinkedIn profile. Student clubs also use Instagram and WhatsApp for more informal event communication.

Does ENSA Fès participate in international academic weeks? ENSA Fès participates in or organizes international academic weeks as part of USMBA's internationalization strategy. These events invite foreign professors and researchers for intensive workshops, joint seminars, and student exchange activities, enhancing the school's global academic connections.

What student-led events are typically organized at ENSA Fès? Student-led events at ENSA Fès include: hackathons and coding contests (Computer Science Club), robotics showcases (Robotics Club), startup pitch competitions (Entrepreneurship Club), cultural evenings (Cultural Club), sports tournaments (Sports Club), and eco-awareness campaigns (Sustainable Development Club).

Does ENSA Fès organize orientation days for new students? Yes. ENSA Fès holds an orientation day (journée d'accueil) at the start of each academic year for newly enrolled CPI students. This event includes introductions to departments, clubs, and services, a campus tour, and informational sessions about academic regulations and student resources.

Does ENSA Fès host competitions for Moroccan high school students? ENSA Fès may organize or participate in outreach events for talented high school students, such as science fairs, olympiad preparatory workshops, or open campus days (journées portes ouvertes). These events aim to promote scientific vocations and showcase ENSA Fès to future applicants.

Has ENSA Fès organized open campus days (portes ouvertes)? Yes. ENSA Fès participates in or organizes open campus days (journées portes ouvertes) where high school students and their families can visit the school, meet faculty and current students, tour the facilities, and learn about available programs and admission requirements.

What was the Game & Tech Summit at ENSA Fès? The Game & Tech Summit was a student event organized at ENSA Fès on December 6, 2025. It featured esports tournaments in Football, FIFA, Valorant, Chess, and Billiards, combined with technology showcases and networking activities for students and tech enthusiasts.

Does ENSA Fès organize robotics competitions? Yes. The ENSA Fès Robotics Club organizes and participates in robotics competitions, both internal (within the school) and national (inter-university). These competitions challenge teams to design, build, and program robots that complete specific tasks.

Does ENSA Fès organize events in collaboration with the national government or ministerial bodies? Yes. ENSA Fès participates in events organized by the Moroccan Ministry of Higher Education, national engineering associations, and USMBA, including national science fairs, accreditation hearings, inter-university conferences, and national innovation challenges.

Are there joint events between ENSA Fès and other USMBA faculties? Yes. ENSA Fès occasionally co-organizes events with other USMBA institutions such as the Faculty of Sciences and Technology (FST) and the Faculty of Letters. These may include inter-faculty sports competitions, cultural festivals, and joint scientific symposia.

What is the frequency of academic conferences at ENSA Fès? ENSA Fès hosts or co-hosts academic conferences several times per year, typically in spring and fall semesters. These events bring together faculty, researchers, and industry experts around specific themes in engineering, AI, energy, or cybersecurity. Proceedings are sometimes published in indexed journals.

Where can I find the latest ENSA Fès event calendar? The latest ENSA Fès event calendar is available on the official website at https://ensaf.ac.ma/calendar.php. Event announcements are also posted on the school's Facebook page (https://www.facebook.com/ensaf.ac.ma) and LinkedIn profile. Student club events may be announced through club-specific social media accounts.

What student services does ENSA Fès offer? ENSA Fès offers a range of student services including: academic administration (scolarité), student affairs (housing, scholarships, social support), a library with physical and digital resources, IT services (Wi-Fi, DWS portal), career and internship support, sports facilities, a subsidized university restaurant, and a medical office.

What is the ENSA Fès DWS portal and what services does it provide? The DWS (Digital Workspace for Students) at https://dws.ensaf.ac.ma provides online services for ENSA Fès students including: viewing grades and academic records, managing enrollment, updating personal information, downloading administrative documents, and accessing academic announcements.

What is the student photo requirement for ENSA Fès services? For the student card and administrative purposes at ENSA Fès, students must provide a passport-size photo in 35×45 mm format, with a digital file size smaller than 500 KB. The photo must meet standard requirements (plain background, face clearly visible).

What is the AMO student health insurance service at ENSA Fès? AMO (Assurance Maladie Obligatoire) is Morocco's mandatory health insurance for students at public universities, including ENSA Fès. Students can register for AMO health coverage through the online portal at https://amo.usmba.ac.ma/. It covers a portion of medical, pharmaceutical, and hospital expenses.

How do ENSA Fès students register for AMO health insurance? ENSA Fès students register for AMO health insurance through the USMBA AMO portal at https://amo.usmba.ac.ma/. Registration requires a valid student card and personal identification documents. The insurance is subsidized by the state and provides basic health coverage.

Does ENSA Fès have a library? Yes. ENSA Fès has a library stocked with engineering textbooks, scientific journals, technical manuals, and reference materials. Students can borrow physical books and access digital resources through the USMBA library network. The library is open during academic hours.

Does ENSA Fès offer IT support services to students? ENSA Fès provides IT support including campus Wi-Fi access, technical assistance for the DWS portal, and access to computer labs equipped with engineering software for coursework. Students experiencing technical issues can contact the school's IT department.

Is there a medical service or nurse's office at ENSA Fès? ENSA Fès or its affiliated USMBA campus has access to a medical office (infirmerie) providing basic first aid and health consultations. For more serious health issues, students are referred to the university hospital (CHU) affiliated with USMBA in Fès.

Does ENSA Fès provide career counseling services? Yes. ENSA Fès's career and internship service provides career counseling including CV review, interview preparation workshops, guidance on internship and job search strategies, and professional networking events. Students are encouraged to use these services from Year 1 of the engineering cycle.

Does ENSA Fès have a university restaurant? Yes. A subsidized university restaurant (restaurant universitaire) is available to ENSA Fès students at very low prices (under 10 Dirhams per meal). It is accessible with a valid student card and offers daily meals during weekdays.

What housing services are available to ENSA Fès students? ENSA Fès students from other cities can apply for a place in the university student residence (cité universitaire) affiliated with USMBA. Applications are submitted at the beginning of the academic year through the student affairs service. Accommodation is heavily subsidized by the Moroccan state.

Does ENSA Fès have a sports facility? ENSA Fès has sports facilities available to students for physical activities and sports club practice. These include outdoor fields for football and basketball. For more specialized facilities, students may access USMBA's broader sports infrastructure on the university campus.

Does ENSA Fès provide scholarship application support? Yes. The student affairs service at ENSA Fès assists students with scholarship applications, including information on available scholarships, eligibility criteria, application deadlines, and required documents. Both merit-based and need-based scholarships are available through the Ministry of Higher Education.

Is there a psychological support service for ENSA Fès students? ENSA Fès, through USMBA, may provide access to psychological support or counseling services for students experiencing academic stress, anxiety, or personal difficulties. Students are encouraged to speak with their program coordinator or the student affairs service to be directed to available support.

What administrative documents can students get from the ENSA Fès scolarité office? The scolarité office at ENSA Fès issues: enrollment certificates, academic transcripts, attestations of results, student card renewal, PFE authorization letters, and graduation-related documents. Processing times vary and students should allow a few business days for most documents.

Does ENSA Fès offer printing or photocopying services to students? ENSA Fès typically has printing and photocopying facilities available on campus, often near the library or the administrative building. Some student clubs and associations also provide printing services. Costs are minimal and paid per page.

Does ENSA Fès offer parking facilities? ENSA Fès has a campus area with parking space available for students, faculty, and staff. Given the school's location on Route d'Imouzzer, many students also commute by bus or taxi from the Fès city center.

Does ENSA Fès provide access to licensed engineering software for students? Yes. ENSA Fès provides access to licensed engineering software tools in its computer labs, including development environments (Eclipse, IntelliJ, Visual Studio), simulation tools (MATLAB, COMSOL), design software (AutoCAD, SolidWorks), and AI/data science tools (Jupyter, TensorFlow environments). Access may also be available remotely through the school's IT infrastructure.

Does ENSA Fès have a dedicated career fair service? Yes. ENSA Fès organizes an annual career and internship fair (forum emploi/stage) as part of its career support services. This event brings together students and representatives from dozens of Moroccan and international companies. The career service manages logistics, communication, and student preparation.

Can ENSA Fès students access e-learning platforms? Yes. ENSA Fès students can access e-learning resources through the school's online platforms, including course materials uploaded by professors, online exercises, and potentially platforms like Moodle. USMBA's digital infrastructure also provides access to national and international e-learning tools.

Is there a lost and found service at ENSA Fès? Lost and found inquiries at ENSA Fès are handled through the administrative office or security desk at the school entrance. Students who find or lose items on campus should report to the school administration.

Does ENSA Fès provide any services for students with disabilities? ENSA Fès, as a public institution, is committed to accessibility. Students with disabilities should contact the student affairs service to discuss available accommodations such as extended exam time, adapted facilities, or other support mechanisms provided under Moroccan higher education accessibility policies.

Are prayer spaces available on the ENSA Fès campus? Yes. ENSA Fès campus has a prayer room (salle de prière) available for Muslim students in compliance with Moroccan cultural norms. The prayer space is accessible during campus hours.

What is the student ID card used for at ENSA Fès? The ENSA Fès student card is used for: accessing campus facilities, borrowing library books, obtaining student discounts on public transport, accessing the university restaurant, proving student status for administrative purposes, and accessing the AMO health insurance services.

Does ENSA Fès have a student information desk? Yes. ENSA Fès has a student information and reception desk (accueil) at the entrance to the school. Staff at the desk provide directions, basic administrative information, and contact details for various services. It is the first point of contact for visitors and prospective students.

Does ENSA Fès have a research support service? Yes. ENSA Fès supports research activities through: departmental research laboratories, access to scientific databases via USMBA's library network, grants for conference participation, and administrative support for national and international research project applications.

Is the ENSA Fès DWS accessible via mobile devices? The DWS portal at https://dws.ensaf.ac.ma is accessible from any internet-connected device, including smartphones and tablets. Students can check grades, update personal data, and access academic documents from their mobile devices using the portal's web interface.

What administrative services can students access on the ENSA Fès website? On the official ENSA Fès website (https://ensaf.ac.ma), students can access: academic calendar, program descriptions, event announcements, news updates, tender publications, application guidelines, and links to the DWS portal and AMO insurance registration.

Does ENSA Fès have a cooperation with employment agencies or public employment services? ENSA Fès may collaborate with Moroccan public employment services (ANAPEC) and private recruitment agencies to facilitate student job placement. Information on job offers and employment support programs may be shared through the school's career service.

Does ENSA Fès offer translation or document certification services? ENSA Fès can provide official certified copies of academic documents (enrollment certificates, transcripts) bearing the school's stamp. Full translation services are not provided in-house; students needing certified translations should use a registered translator or notary, especially for international applications.
